rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HONG CHAN AND LIN CHEW CHAN LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Evidence Sanction Against Petitioners Julie And Robert Chan, Supporting Declaration Of Ross Mclauran Madden, Separate Statement, And Supporting Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tueday, September 30, 2014, Line 4, RESPONDENT DARRYL CHAN Motion For Order Imposing Evidence Sanction Against Petitioners Julie And Robert Chan, Supporting Declaration Of Ross Mclauran Madden, Separate Statement, And Supporting Memorandum. Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. The motion was served on September 23, 2014 via e-mail. This motion requires 16 court days notice plus 2 additional calendar days for e-mail service. See CCP Section 1005(b). Moving party failed to obtain a court order shortening time for service. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CP III RINCON TOWERS, INC., A COLORADO CORPORATION VS. ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY ANAD COUNTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Amended Petition For Writ On Mandate And/Or Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 30, 2014, Line 14, PETITIONER CP III RINCON TOWERS, INC.'s Amended Petition For Writ On Mandate And/Or Writ Of Administrative Mandate. The court denies the petition for writ of mandate/administrative mandamus. Petitioner fails to demonstrate that respondent abused its discretion. First, petitioner is not an affected party within the meaning of CA Revenue and Taxation Code sec. 1603 or 18 CCR 301(g). Petitioner acquired the property after the lien date and has not shown that it is responsible for the tax payments. Respondent's interpretation of the governing codes/regulations is reasonable and the court defers to that interpretation. See Los Altos El Granada Investors v. City of Capitola (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 629, 648. Petitioner has not shown that respondent had a duty to allow it to substitute into the administrative proceeding. Second, the reasoning of IBM Personal Pension Plan v. CCSF (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 1291, 1305, and the federal tax scheme (31 USC 3727 and In re Freeman, 489 F.2d 431, 433 (9th Cir. 1973)) show that respondent did not abuse its discretion. Respondent has no interest or duty to become embroiled in the dispute between petitioner and the real party in interest. Third, petitioner does not demonstrate that writ relief is necessary. Petitioner has a remedy at law-it can pursue its rights against the real party in interest in a separate action. Lastly, petitioner argues that the dispute with the real party in interest is resolved, but the foreclosure action (case CGC-10-496887) is currently on appeal. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES O'NEAL VS. ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC A CALIFORNIA CORP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tueday, September 30, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CHARLES O'NEAL Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's objection to Aleksander A. Vokov's Declaration is overruled. Plaintiff failed to provide a separate reply for this motion. Plaintiff's Motion is granted and denied as follow: Interrogatories #3.1, 15.1, 17.1, 50.1-50.6: Granted. Defendant to provide a further response to each and every subpart. The code requires that a party must make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th1496 "A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under his control." Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771,782. This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Sup. Ct (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA 2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (U.Z.MFG.Co) (1984) 161 CA 3d 15,167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (Benny) (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts. Sigerseth v. Superior Court(1972) 23 CA 3d 427,433. See Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2014) 8:1051-1060 Interrogatories #4.1, 12.1, 16.6 : Denied. Defendant's objections on the grounds of vague and ambiguous as to the term "Incident" is sustained. This wage and hour claim consists of multiple events and a definition of the word "Incident" is necessary. This ruling is without prejudice. Defendant to serve verified responses to Form Interrogatories (General) Interrogatories #3.1, 15.1, 17.1, 50.1-50.6 no later than October 21, 2014. All requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES O'NEAL VS. ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC A CALIFORNIA CORP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Charles O'Neal'S Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tueday, September 30, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CHARLES O'NEAL Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Charles O'Neal'S Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant. Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's objection to Aleksander A. Vokov's Declaration is overruled. Plaintiff failed to provide a separate reply for this motion. Interrogatories #1, 5, 6, 7: Defendant's objection to the term "worked for" is sustained and modified to describe any person who worked for defendant in an exempt or non-exempt employment capacity. Defendant's objection on the grounds of third party privacy rights is overruled. The names and locations of witnesses are discoverable. See CCP Section 2017.010. Defendant to provide a further verified response to this request. Interrogatories #2, 3: Defendant's objections are overruled. Defendant's objection on the grounds of third party privacy rights is overruled. The names and locations of witnesses are discoverable. See CCP Section 2017.010. Defendant to provide a further verified response to this request. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted against defendant and his attorneys of record in the amount of $1560.00 made payable to plaintiff no later than October 21, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES O'NEAL VS. ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC A CALIFORNIA CORP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tueday, September 30, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CHARLES O'NEAL Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's objection to Aleksander A. Vokov's Declaration is overruled. Plaintiff failed to provide a separate reply for this motion. Plaintiff's motion is denied. Interrogatories 216.1 and 217.1 are duplicates of 16.1 and 17.1 of the Form Interrogatories. The pro tem finds that Plaintiff's service of these interrogatories was in bad faith. Defendants' request for sanctions is granted against plaintiff and his attorney of record in the amount of $617.50 made payable to defendant no later than October 21, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES O'NEAL VS. ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC A CALIFORNIA CORP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Charles O'Neal'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tueday, September 30, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CHARLES O'NEAL Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Charles O'Neal'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant. Pro Tem Judge Kathy Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's objection to Aleksander A. Vokov's Declaration is overruled. Plaintiff failed to provide a separate reply for this motion. Defendant's objection on the grounds that the term "employment" calls for a legal conclusion is overruled. The pro-tem is not able to rule on the claims of financial privacy, privacy of third-parties, claims of work product and attorney client privilege without a privilege log. Defendants to serve a further response in compliance with CCP Section 2031.220-240 which includes a privilege log if any document is being withheld and produce documents no later than October 21, 2014. Defendant need not list in the privilege log any attorney-client communications or absolute work product created after the claim was filed with the Labor and Workforce Development Agency. Plaintiff may refile this motion once he receives plaintiff's further response and production of documents. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted against defendant and his attorneys of record in the amount of $1560.00 made payable to plaintiff no later than October 21, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
